Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to analyze a provided event log for a Procure to Pay P2P process identify anomalies unusual or anomalous behavior relative to standard P2P sequences and explain why these anomalies are problematic based on general business knowledge and best practices

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Standard P2P Process Step 1 Create Purchase Requisition PR Step 2 Approve Purchase Requisition PR Approval Step 3 Issue Purchase Order PO Step 4 Receive Goods or Services Goods Receipt GR Step 5 Vendor Invoice Received Step 6 Match Invoice to PO and GR 3 way matching Step 7 Release Payment Step 8 Close Case Key Rules PR must be approved before a PO is issued PO must be issued before goods are received Invoice matching usually happens after the invoice is received and goods are received Payment should only be released after successful invoice matching and usually after goods receipt

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze Each Case in the Event Log Case 1001 Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Receive Goods Vendor Invoice Received Match Invoice to PO Release Payment Close Case Analysis This follows the standard ideal P2P flow perfectly No anomalies

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002 Create PR Issue PO Vendor Invoice Received Release Payment Approve PR Receive Goods Match Invoice to PO Close Case Anomalies PO issued before PR approval Payment released before PR approval Goods Receipt and Invoice Matching Invoice received and paid before goods are received

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Why problematic High risk of fraud paying for goods not received bypassing budgetary controls This is a classic maverick buying or potential fraud scenario

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Release Payment Vendor Invoice Received Receive Goods Match Invoice to PO Close Case Anomalies Payment released before Invoice Received Payment released before Goods Receipt

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Why problematic Paying without an invoice or goods receipt violates the 3 way match principle It exposes the company to financial loss if the vendor fails to deliver or overcharges

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1004 Create PR Approve PR Receive Goods Issue PO Vendor Invoice Received Match Invoice to PO Release Payment Close Case Anomalies Goods received before PO is issued

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Why problematic Receiving goods without a valid PO means the company has no contractual agreement on price quantity or terms at the time of delivery It can lead to disputes overcharging or accepting unordered goods While rush orders happen systematically it is a control failure

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Structure the Response Introduction Briefly state the standard P2P flow to establish the baseline for comparison Case by case analysis detail the anomalies found in Cases 1002 1003 and 1004 Note Case 1001 is normal mention it briefly as the baseline For each anomaly state the specific out of order sequence and explain why it is problematic financial risk compliance fraud operational inefficiency Summary Conclusion provide a high level summary of the risks such as lack of 3 way matching bypassing approvals and recommend process improvements such as system hard stops enforcing 3 way match before payment

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self Correction during drafting Make sure to explicitly mention the Three Way Match PO Goods Receipt Invoice as it is the cornerstone of P2P controls Highlight that Case 1002 is the most severe due to the complete inversion of the control flow

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refine the Output Ensure professional tone clear headings and bullet points for readability Check timestamps to ensure accuracy in the explanation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Polish Reviewing the generated response against the prompt requirements The response identifies anomalies explains why they are problematic and relies on standard P2P business practices Ready to output
